name: Compare 2
description: Dual analogue window comparator with logic section. Each side detects whether the input voltage sits inside a shift/size-defined window. Extracts gates and pulses from any CV or audio signal.

controls.name: SHIFT
controls.description: Offsets the detection window's centre voltage.

controls.name: SIZE
controls.description: Sets distance between the window's lower and upper edges.
controls.detail: ~few mV to 10V · negative after adding CV disables the comparator

inputs.name: SHIFT CV
inputs.description: Modulates window centre. Sums with the SHIFT knob. Processes bipolar CV.

inputs.name: SIZE CV
inputs.description: Modulates window size. Sums with the SIZE knob. Negative sum disables comparator output.

inputs.name: IN
inputs.description: Analogue signal to compare. Accepts CV or audio.

outputs.name: OUT
outputs.description: Main gate: high when the input sits inside the detection window.

outputs.name: NOT
outputs.description: Inverted main gate. High whenever OUT is low.

outputs.name: AND
outputs.description: Logic AND of both comparators. High only when both are inside their windows.

outputs.name: OR
outputs.description: Logic OR of both comparators. High when either or both are inside their windows.

outputs.name: XOR
outputs.description: Logic XOR of both comparators. High only when exactly one is inside its window.

outputs.name: FF
outputs.description: T flip-flop. Toggles state on every rising edge of XOR.

behaviors.name: Window detection
behaviors.description: OUT goes high while the input voltage sits between the two window edges. LED shows blue below window, red above, white inside.

behaviors.name: Negative window
behaviors.trigger: SIZE knob + CV sum goes below 0
behaviors.description: Comparator output stays low permanently. LED extinguishes rather than turning white. Useful for CV-gated muting of a gate stream.

behaviors.name: Left-to-right normalisation
behaviors.description: IN, SHIFT CV and SIZE CV all normalise from the left comparator to the right. Lets a single signal or CV drive both windows simultaneously.

behaviors.name: XOR flip-flop
behaviors.description: Every rising XOR edge toggles the FF output. Divides XOR rate by two; transitions OUT-based pulse train into sub-octave or latched gate.

behaviors.name: Set/reset latch
behaviors.description: Per comparator, patching OUT back to SIZE CV with SHIFT min and SIZE ~3 o'clock turns it into an SR latch. SHIFT CV rising edge sets, IN rising edge resets.

behaviors.name: Audio-rate PWM
behaviors.description: At audio rate the comparator emits a pulse wave. SHIFT moves phase/width, SIZE sets duty. Output can jump between fundamental and +1 octave as the window clips both halves.

behaviors.name: Logic input thresholds
behaviors.description: With SHIFT max and SIZE noon, window is +2.5V to +7.5V. All five jacks (IN, SHIFT CV, SIZE CV × 2) then accept standard +5V gates, yielding a 6-input logic array.

patchIdeas.name: Complex rhythm generator
patchIdeas.patch: LFO → left IN · dial SHIFT/SIZE per side to carve gate patterns at different points of the LFO cycle · AND/OR/XOR/FF outputs drive envelopes or drums · modulate SHIFT/SIZE CV to evolve the rhythm
patchIdeas.diagram:
Compare 2:
* CH 1 SHIFT: taste
* CH 1 SIZE: narrow
* CH 2 SHIFT: different offset
* CH 2 SIZE: medium

- LFO (Out) >> Compare 2 (IN) // normalises to right
- Compare 2 (AND) g> Envelope 1 (Trig)
- Compare 2 (XOR) g> Drum (Trig)
- Compare 2 (FF) g> Envelope 2 (Gate) // half-rate latch

patchIdeas.name: Audio frequency multiplier
patchIdeas.patch: Triangle VCO → left IN (normalised to right) · both comparators on · XOR output as audio · SHIFT/SIZE sweep output between fundamental, +1 and +2 octave with complex pulse waveforms · use FF for −1/0/+1 octave jumps
patchIdeas.diagram:
Compare 2:
* CH 1 SHIFT: noon
* CH 1 SIZE: narrow
* CH 2 SHIFT: offset
* CH 2 SIZE: narrow

- VCO (Tri) -> Compare 2 (IN)
- Compare 2 (XOR) -> Mixer (In) // pulse, octave-shifting with SIZE
- Compare 2 (FF) -> Mixer (In) // sub-octave variant

patchIdeas.name: Digital ring modulator
patchIdeas.patch: VCO A → left IN · VCO B → right IN · XOR output as audio · detune sources for ring mod timbres · modulate SHIFT/SIZE for PWM-style bite
patchIdeas.diagram:
Compare 2:
* CH 1 SHIFT: 0V
* CH 1 SIZE: narrow
* CH 2 SHIFT: 0V
* CH 2 SIZE: narrow

- VCO A (Out) -> Compare 2.CH 1 (IN)
- VCO B (Out) -> Compare 2.CH 2 (IN)
- LFO (Out) >> Compare 2.CH 1 (SIZE CV) // PWM on ring mod
- Compare 2 (XOR) -> VCA (In)

patchIdeas.name: Dynamic depth VCO sync
patchIdeas.patch: Slave VCO → CH 1 IN · sync source VCO → CH 2 IN · XOR → slave's FM input (depth knob on VCO or VCA) · depth and Compare 2 settings set sync timbre · VCA in FM path for voltage-controlled sync amount
patchIdeas.diagram:
Compare 2:
* CH 1 SHIFT/SIZE: narrow window near 0V
* CH 2 SHIFT/SIZE: same

- Slave VCO (Out) -> Compare 2.CH 1 (IN)
- Sync VCO (Out) -> Compare 2.CH 2 (IN)
- Compare 2 (XOR) >> VCA (In)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)
- VCA (Out) >> Slave VCO (FM)

patchIdeas.name: Voltage-controlled swing
patchIdeas.patch: Clock → left IN · LFO → SHIFT CV · fixed narrow SIZE · slow LFO shifts detection threshold, producing a clock whose edges shift earlier/later — swing that can be CV'd
patchIdeas.diagram:
Compare 2:
* CH 1 SHIFT: 0V
* CH 1 SIZE: narrow

- Clock (Out) c> Compare 2 (IN)
- LFO (Out) >> Compare 2 (SHIFT CV) // swing depth
- Compare 2 (OUT) g> Drum (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Set/reset latch
patchIdeas.patch: SHIFT min · SIZE ~3 o'clock · OUT → SIZE CV (self-feedback) · rising edge on SHIFT CV sets the latch high · rising edge on IN resets it low · LED reports latch state
patchIdeas.diagram:
Compare 2:
* CH 1 SHIFT: min
* CH 1 SIZE: ~3 o'clock

- Compare 2 (OUT) >> Compare 2 (SIZE CV) // latch feedback
- Set Gate (Out) g> Compare 2 (SHIFT CV)
- Reset Gate (Out) g> Compare 2 (IN)

patchIdeas.name: Six-input logic array
patchIdeas.patch: SHIFT max · SIZE noon (window +2.5V to +7.5V) · any +5V gate on IN, SHIFT CV or SIZE CV activates that comparator · AND/OR/XOR/FF combine gate streams from up to six sources
patchIdeas.diagram:
Compare 2:
* CH 1 SHIFT: max
* CH 1 SIZE: noon
* CH 2 SHIFT: max
* CH 2 SIZE: noon

- Gate A (Out) g> Compare 2.CH 1 (IN)
- Gate B (Out) g> Compare 2.CH 1 (SHIFT CV)
- Gate C (Out) g> Compare 2.CH 1 (SIZE CV)
- Gate D (Out) g> Compare 2.CH 2 (IN)
- Compare 2 (AND) g> Drum (Trig)
- Compare 2 (XOR) g> Envelope (Trig)
